name: Girl - circle by San Lois
region_intro: Jogging in Esteiro Do Tambre offers diverse landscapes, from the extensive estuarine environment of the Tambre river mouth to lush riverside forests. The region features intertidal muddy and sandy spaces, reed beds, and both mobile and fixed dunes, providing varied terrains for runners. Further inland, mountainous slopes covered with forests and agricultural land offer additional running routes. This area is characterized by its well-preserved natural features, including coastal lagoons, coves, and shallow bays. Best jogging routes in Esteiro Do Tambre The most popular jogging route is Girl - circle by San Lois, a 7.3 miles (11.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 25 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Río Vilacoba in Noia – Noia Historic Town and Harbor loop from Noia, a moderate 7.3 miles (11.7 km) path. This route explores the historic town and harbor of Noia along the Río Vilacoba. Local runners also love the Running loop from Outes, a 4.8 miles (7.7 km) trail leading through the scenic area around Outes, often completed in about 49 minutes. Jogging in Esteiro Do Tambre is defined by its estuarine environment, riverside forests, and coastal paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy routes to more challenging trails. The routes in Esteiro Do Tambre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 470 runners have used komoot to explore Esteiro Do Tambre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Esteiro Do Tambre?
A: Esteiro Do Tambre offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 40 trails available on komoot. These routes cater to various fitness levels, including 1 easy, 31 moderate, and 11 difficult options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Esteiro Do Tambre?
A: The region boasts diverse terrain for runners. You'll find extensive estuarine environments with intertidal muddy and sandy spaces, reed beds, and both mobile and fixed dunes. Beyond the coast, routes extend into lush riverside forests with species like alder and ash, and further inland, mountainous slopes covered with forests and agricultural land provide varied views and challenges.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners in Esteiro Do Tambre?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy running route available in Esteiro Do Tambre. While many trails are moderate, beginners can find suitable paths to enjoy the region's natural beauty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38250615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miradoiro do Paseo Marítimo loop from Noia&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.7 km route with minimal elevation gain, making it a good option for those looking for a less strenuous run.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Esteiro Do Tambre?
A: The running trails in Esteiro Do Tambre are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an impressive average rating of 4.8 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 470 runners have explored the varied terrain here, often praising the well-preserved natural features and the diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Esteiro Do Tambre?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Esteiro Do Tambre are designed as loops, offering convenient circular paths. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31499854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Vilacoba in Noia – Noia Historic Town and Harbor loop from Noia&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 11.7 km route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Outes&lt;/a&gt;, a 7.7 km moderate trail.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while running in Esteiro Do Tambre?
A: Jogging in Esteiro Do Tambre offers views of significant natural features such as the extensive estuarine environment of the Tambre river mouth, reed beds, and coastal lagoons. You can also explore lush riverside forests. A notable historical landmark is the medieval Ponte Nafonso, a bridge with twenty arches, located where the Tambre river meets the municipalities of Noia and Outes.

Q: Is Esteiro Do Tambre suitable for family-friendly running or walking?
A: Yes, the diverse landscape of Esteiro Do Tambre includes many accessible paths that are suitable for families. While some routes are more challenging, there are easy hiking options and trails with varied terrains that can be enjoyed by different fitness levels, making it a great area for family outings.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Esteiro Do Tambre?
A: Esteiro Do Tambre is a natural area, and many trails are generally dog-friendly. However, as it is a Special Zone of Conservation (ZEC), it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with sensitive wildlife or near agricultural land, to protect the local ecosystem and ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: What is the longest dedicated greenway for running in the region?
A: The Vía Verde Compostela-Tambre-Lengüelle is Galicia's longest greenway, stretching 28.36 km. This dedicated route offers an extensive and scenic option for joggers and cyclists, providing a continuous path through varied landscapes.

Q: Are there any coastal running routes in Esteiro Do Tambre?
A: Yes, the region's estuarine environment and coastal features provide excellent opportunities for coastal running. You can find paths alongside coastal lagoons, coves, and shallow bays. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24608365" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia da Telleira – Praia da Ornanda loop from Taramancos&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.7 km route that takes you along the coast.

Q: When is the best time of year to go running in Esteiro Do Tambre?
A: Esteiro Do Tambre offers pleasant running conditions throughout much of the year. The diverse landscapes, including shaded riverside forests, can provide relief during warmer months. During winter, the area is known for congregating aquatic birds, adding a unique element to your run, though appropriate gear for cooler, potentially wetter weather would be advisable.
tips: River walk and recreational area next to the Vilacoba river in Noia crossing on the south side towards the mountain area of San Lois
Recreational area above the Noia. The summit at 380 m above sea level offers spectacular views towards the estuary and the Atlantic Ocean. The top is marked by telecommunication towers

waypoints.name: View of the Noia Estuary from Alto de San Lois
